title: Resources
text:
About the workshop
There is no cost to attend the event. However, spaces are limited and we encourage early registration of interest. We may be able to provide some financial support to cover travel expenses from outside of London.


Workshop materials
All workshop materials will be made available on this website after the event.


Contact
Please email katharine.sherratt@lshtm.ac.uk for information and support with access or adjustments.

title: Evaluation & responsibility in infectious disease modelling
text: 37 participants, contributing up to 3 keywords to describe responsible/irresponsible modelling1. Word size represents frequency; coloured blue for responsible, orange for irresponsible.

title: Evaluation & responsibility in infectious disease modelling
text:
Footnotes


Using up to 3 keywords, what does “responsible”/“irresponsible” modelling look like for you?↩︎

title: How do we evaluate modelling work, and what does that imply about modelling responsibly?
text:
NoteKey details




24 March 2026, 10.00–16.30
LSHTM, Keppel St, London WC1E 7HT
30 participants across academia and public health


Participate
Organised by Kath Sherratt (LSHTM), Seb Funk (LSHTM), and Erica Thompson (UCL), and supported by LSHTM CMMID

title: How do we evaluate modelling work, and what does that imply about modelling responsibly?
text:
Footnotes


Workshop design motivated by principles of structured groupwork.↩︎

title: Participate
text:
We encourage participants to join from any background and experience.
Registration for the workshop is open until 13 March.
We have a limit on the number of participant places so that we can best support group work. We are likely to be oversubscribed and will operate a waitlist after this point.
We will be in touch by 17 March to confirm places. Please let us know as soon as possible if you can no longer attend.






NoteParticipant support





There is no cost to attend.
We will provide lunch and refreshments. We have some funds available to support UK travel, and can make accessibility adjustments wherever possible.
Please email Kath at katharine.sherratt@lshtm.ac.uk for more information and support with travel or accessibility.
We are unable to provide recordings of the workshop. All workshop materials will be available on this website.
